prefixes.label: ch-
prefixes.notes: Hot-temperature bench character (unitary glyph). ch and sh are NOT decomposable into c+h / s+h — they form a single graphemic class with 76.5% suffix overlap with each other, vs only 11.1% with bare s-. Near-parity with sh- in Balneo section only.

prefixes.label: sh-
prefixes.notes: Cold-temperature bench character (unitary glyph). sh and ch are NOT decomposable into s+h / c+h. sh- takes processual suffixes (-edy, -eey) at 3.8x the rate bare s- does, confirming they are distinct morphemes.

prefixes.label: da-
prefixes.notes: Medicine / essence prefix. Stable across all sections; ratio C/H approximately 1.00.

prefixes.label: ok-
prefixes.notes: Water-locative prefix: o (in) + k (water). Distinct from qok- (dosed water).

prefixes.label: qok-
prefixes.notes: Dosed-water verbal paradigm: qo (dose) + k (water). 51% of tokens end in -y (processual).

prefixes.label: qot-
prefixes.notes: Dosed-oil verbal paradigm: qo (dose) + t (oil). qotch/qotsh ratio ~12:1 (more hot-biased than water).

prefixes.label: ot-
prefixes.notes: Passive / reflexive prefix. Enriched 2.43x in Zodiac (155.0 permille vs corpus 63.7 permille). Exclude tokens starting with 'oth'.

prefixes.label: l-
prefixes.notes: Spirit (alcohol) prefix. l = alcohol/spirit univocally (no positional disambiguation). Enriched 1.97x vs corpus baseline in Stars section (73.2 permille vs corpus 37.1 permille).

prefixes.label: y-
prefixes.notes: Imperative prefix. Enriched ~2x in Cosmo section.

prefixes.label: s-
prefixes.notes: Vinegar / cold-acetic prefix. IMPORTANT: must exclude tokens starting with 'sh' to avoid conflating with the unitary bench character sh-. s- carries processual suffixes at 7.4% (mostly compound forms) vs nominal suffixes at 36.5%. Note: s- = cold/acetic on the same Galenic axis; context determines whether 'vinegar' or 'cold quality' is the better gloss.

prefixes.label: t-
prefixes.notes: Oil prefix. Exclude tokens starting with 'th'. qotch/qotsh ~12:1 reflects hot extraction bias for oils.

corpus_notes.standalone_matching: All prefix counts use standalone word-boundary matching on full tokens. Substring matching is never used.
corpus_notes.uncertainty_marker: '!' characters within tokens indicate uncertain readings; strip before analysis.
corpus_notes.bench_characters: ch and sh are unitary glyphs (bench characters), not sequences of c+h or s+h. They share 76.5% suffix overlap with each other but only 11.1% with bare s-. Do not decompose them morphemically.
